steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content: The student needs to contrast the subject matter of the two prints—what each work depicts or expresses as its theme—not how they were made or when.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "The print by Salazar is a black-and-white linocut; by contrast, Shikongo's is a relief print.": Contrasts printmaking technique, not subject matter. ✗
  • "The scenes depicted in both works were first carved onto a printing block, then stamped onto paper; however, one work is a linocut, while the other is a relief print.": Also focuses on technique and production method. ✗
  • "Salazar made *La Protesta* ("The Protest") in 1937, while Shikongo made *The Love Is Approaching* later, in 1974.": Contrasts dates of creation, not subject matter. ✗
  • "Salazar's print expresses an explicitly political point of view, while Shikongo's depicts a tranquil, everyday scene.": Directly contrasts what each print is about—political expression versus an everyday scene. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
